items.headline: Worksafe looking into Elijah Hollands' MCG episode
items.description: Carlton's management of Elijah Hollands' public mental health episode will come under scrutiny from Worksafe Victoria.

items.headline: Leody Taveras hits first career grand slam and Orioles hold on for wild 7-5 win over Royals in 12
items.description: — Leody Taveras hit a grand slam during a five-run 12th inning, and the Baltimore Orioles held on for a wild 7-5 victory over the Royals on Monday night, handing Kansas City its eighth straight loss.

items.headline: Muncy, Rushing both homer twice as Dodgers rout Rockies 12-3 behind Wrobleski to split 4-game set
items.description: — Max Muncy went 4 for 4 with two homers and scored four times as the Los Angeles Dodgers hit five longballs to back Justin Wrobleski in a 12-3 victory Monday night over the Colorado Rockies.

items.headline: Martinook rescues Hurricanes in 2OT after overturned goal
items.description: Jordan Martinook, who stopped on a penalty shot in the first overtime session after a potential winning goal was overturned, beat Linus Ullmark at 13:53 of the second overtime to help the Carolina Hurricanes past the Ottawa Senators 3-2 on Monday.

items.headline: Stanley Cup playoffs 2026: Bracket, schedule, scores, news
items.description: From the first round all the way through the Stanley Cup Final, ESPN has you covered this NHL postseason.

items.headline: GT's middle order 'exposed', but the problem might be higher up
items.description: "We shouldn't be allowing Rahul Tewatia or Shahrukh Khan [to face] lots of balls," Matthew Hayden, Gujarat Titans' batting coach, says after the collapse against Mumbai Indians

items.headline: Recall Kerevi: Genia's 'power' advice for Wallabies' World Cup run
items.description: Believing "size matters", former Wallabies captain Will Genia would love to see Samu Kerevi form an explosive midfield pairing with Joseph-Aukuso Suaalii at next year's Rugby World Cup.

items.headline: Chennaiyin FC host Mohammedan Sporting Club in ISL: Indian Sports LIVE, April 21
items.description: Here are all the updates from Indian sports on April 21, 2026.

items.headline: CJ McCollum leads late rally as Hawks stun Knicks to tie series
items.description: CJ McCollum scored 32 points, and the Hawks rallied to stun the Knicks 107-106 on Monday night, tying their first-round playoff series at one game apiece.

items.headline: CJ McCollum leads a late rally as the Hawks stun the Knicks 107-106 to tie their series at 1-1
items.description: — CJ McCollum scored 32 points and the Atlanta Hawks rallied to stun the New York Knicks 107-106 on Monday night, tying their first-round playoff series at one game apiece.

items.headline: 2026 NBA playoffs: Schedule, scores, news and highlights
items.description: The Eastern and Western Conference playoff brackets are set after Sunday's games. See the full schedule of games.

items.headline: Rea and Swanson help the Cubs beat the slumping Phillies 5-1 for their 6th straight win
items.description: — Colin Rea pitched into the seventh inning and the Chicago Cubs beat the Philadelphia Phillies 5-1 on Monday night for their sixth consecutive win.

items.headline: Martone, 19, scores again; Flyers take 2-0 lead on Penguins
items.description: Porter Martone, 19, became the first teenager in Flyers franchise history to record a goal in each of his first two playoff games.

items.headline: Gill misses chance to top Orange Cap table, Rabada on the rise on Purple Cap list
items.description: Here's what the Orange and Purple Cap tables at IPL 2026 look like after Mumbai Indians' dominant win over Gujarat Titans on Monday
